id3: Assessment of the Welfare Measures available to War Widows/Families in Armed Forces
opn1: People living near the Line of Actual Control are considered the first line of defence. They need to be incentivised to stay in these areas, and development under the Vibrant Villages Programme was essential for this. People now visiting the border area know Kibithoo (a border village in Arunachal Pradesh) not as the last village but as the first village in India.
opnn: On the occasion of launch of Vibrant Villages Programme

id3: Assessment of the Welfare Measures available to War Widows/Families in Armed Forces
nws: Vibrant Villages Programme has been launched.
pnt1: The ?4,800 crores Vibrant Villages Programme (VVP) is to be executed in 2,967 villages across 19 districts in four border States, including Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim, Uttarakhand and Himachal Pradesh, and the union territory of Ladakh. Under this programme the development work of villages will be done at three levels: First, there will not be a single house in the border villages which does not have basic amenities. The second goal of this program is to stop migration from border villages, for which employment arrangements will be made there. The third objective of this program is to set up basic infrastructure in the border villages.

id3: Role of regulators, auditors, rating agencies and other stakeholders in achieving world-class corporate governance
texp: Outcome-focused regulation focuses on the outcomes of a process rather than how to achieve the outcomes. Traditionally, regulation directly mandates or prohibits action by firms by enforcement of certain norms and practices. It generally requires an organization to ensure that its processes are compliant. The outcome-focused regulations, on the other hand, influence action by stimulating other forms of adaptation such as process or product innovation and by indirectly influencing stakeholder behaviour. Small firms experience indirect regulatory influences most commonly as changes in the cost or availability of resources, or in changing product sales. Recently in the service industry, outcome-focused regulations have been gaining prominence.

id3: Review of National Council for Teacher Education in the context of Teachers’ Training/Capacity Building
texp: The correlation between teacher certification conferred by colleges of education and a teacher’s effect on student academic outcomes has been found to be minimal. Teachers these days are viewed more as decision-makers and reflective practitioners. This demands that teacher education move away from a curriculum focused on what teachers need to know to a curriculum organized around core practices, in which knowledge, skill, and professional identity. This requires us to add pedagogies of enactment to our existing repertoire of pedagogies of reflection and investigation. In order to make this shift, teacher educators will need to undo a number of historical divisions that underlie the education of teachers. This demands a case for teacher re-education.
